title: poll_once log hides which issue sync skipped and why (silent never-worked issues)
description:
## Problem

When `sync_issues` skips an issue, the only signal a scheduled `poll_once` pass
leaves is a bare **count** in the poll log:

```
... | sync: synced: +0 new story(ies), 1 skipped | ran 0 ticket(s); ...
```

It never says **which** issue was skipped or **why**. A malformed issue (e.g. two
type labels, or no usable body) is therefore silently never-worked — it just rots,
and the operator only notices because "nothing is happening." The reason already
exists: `sync_issues.skip_reason` returns it and `sync_issues.main()` prints it —
but `poll_once` discards the per-issue `(sid, reason)` pairs and logs only
`len(skipped)`.

## Evidence (2026-06-29)

A `system-repair` issue (engine GH-87) was filed with two type labels
(`bug` + `system-repair`). `skip_reason` rejected it ("expected exactly one type
label ... got ['bug', 'system-repair']") on every pass. The poll log showed only
`1 skipped` for hours; the issue never entered the prd and was never worked. The
cause was only found by manually re-running `skip_reason`.

## Proposed fix

Surface skipped issues in the poll-log summary line (bounded, one line as usual):
include each skipped `sid: reason`, or at least the ids, when `skipped` is
non-empty — e.g. `... 1 skipped (GH-87: expected exactly one type label ...)`.
`poll_once.sync_fn` already receives `(added, skipped)` from `pull_and_sync`; it
just needs to thread the reasons into `format_summary_line` instead of dropping
them. Keep the field clipped so one pass stays one tidy line.

## Note

This is observability only — it does not change which issues are worked. A
separate doc fix clarifies that `system-repair` IS the type label (don't add a
second), which is the specific malformation that triggered this.
